content: Use `cursor-tools web "<your question>"` to get answers from the web using Perplexity AI.
When using web for complex queries, it is suggested to write the output to a file (e.g., local-research/<query summary>.md).
Use `cursor-tools repo "<your question>"` to get context-aware answers about this repository using Google Gemini.
Use `cursor-tools doc [options]` to generate comprehensive documentation for this repository.
When using doc for remote repos, it is suggested to write the output to a file (e.g., local-docs/<repo-name>.md).
Use `cursor-tools github pr [number]` or `issue [number]` to get the last 10 PRs/issues, or a specific one by number.
Use `cursor-tools browser open <url> [options]` to open a URL and capture page content, console logs, and network activity.
Use `cursor-tools browser act "<instruction>" --url=<url> [options]` to execute actions on a webpage using natural language instructions.
Use `cursor-tools browser observe "<instruction>" --url=<url> [options]` to observe interactive elements on a webpage and suggest possible actions.
Use `cursor-tools browser extract "<instruction>" --url=<url> [options]` to extract data from a webpage based on natural language instructions.
All browser commands are stateless: each command starts with a fresh browser instance and closes it when done.
Multi-step workflows are supported in the `act` command using the pipe (|) separator.
Video recording is available for all browser commands using the `--video=<directory>` option.
DO NOT ask browser `act` to 'wait' for anything, the wait command is currently disabled.
`cursor-tools web` is best for general web information not specific to the repository.
`cursor-tools repo` is ideal for repository-specific questions, planning, code review and debugging.
`cursor-tools browser` is useful for testing and debugging web apps.
Commands can be run from a local installation or directly via `npx` or `bunx`.
General command options are available to specify an AI model (`--model`), control response length (`--max-tokens`), or save output to a file (`--save-to`).
The `doc` and `github` commands support operating on a remote GitHub repository via the `--from-github` option.

Browser commands support various options to capture content (`--console`, `--html`, `--network`), save artifacts (`--screenshot`), and control browser behavior (`--timeout`, `--viewport`, `--headless`).
Configuration is in `cursor-tools.config.json` and API keys are loaded from `.cursor-tools.env`.
Browser commands require separate installation of Playwright: `npm install --save-dev playwright` or `npm install -g playwright`.
Remember: You're part of a team of superhuman expert AIs. Work together to solve complex problems.
